description: Figure 1 presents log–log plots of weight gain versus time for high-purity Fe–Mn–Al alloys isothermally oxidized at 800°C in pure oxygen (200 torr) for up to 24 hr. Panel (a) shows alloys that form protective alumina scales, with composition-labeled curves (e.g., 10–9, 30–11, 5–8, 20–10, 1–8) exhibiting near-linear behavior; the fitted slopes give exponent values n = 2.9, 3.7, 10.6, 4.6, and 5.6 from the rate equation (ΔW/A)^n = kt, indicating a wide kinetic range rather than simple parabolic oxidation. Panel (b) displays alloys exhibiting breakaway and healing kinetics (e.g., 25–7, 5–6, 7.5–5(58), 20–6(37), 25–6, 1–6), where weight-gain curves are segmented: an initial slow-growth stage is followed by a sharp acceleration (breakaway) and subsequent deceleration (healing), reflecting cyclic failure and reformation of the oxide scale. The figure contrasts stable, low-rate protective oxidation with unstable, high-rate non-protective behavior, supporting the conclusion that oxidation resistance in Fe–Mn–Al alloys is strongly composition-dependent and that simple rate laws poorly describe the breakaway–healing regime.
